en: One day, as Jesus was teaching the people in the temple and preaching the gospel, the chief priests and the scribes with the elders came up
zh: 有一天，耶穌在殿裡教導人，宣講福音，眾祭司長、經學家和長老上前來，

en: and said to him, "Tell us by what authority you do these things, or who it is that gave you this authority."
zh: 對他說：“告訴我們，你憑著甚麼權柄作這些事？給你這權柄的是誰？”

en: He answered them, "I also will ask you a question. Now tell me,
zh: 他回答他們：“我也要問你們一句話，你們告訴我：

en: Was the baptism of John from heaven or from man?"
zh: 約翰的洗禮是從天上來的，還是從人來的呢？”

en: And they discussed it with one another, saying, "If we say, 'From heaven,' he will say, 'Why did you not believe him?'
zh: 他們就彼此議論說：“如果我們說：‘是從天上來的’，他就會問：‘那你們為甚麼不相信他呢？’

en: But if we say, 'From man,' all the people will stone us to death, for they are convinced that John was a prophet."
zh: 如果我們說：‘是從人來的’，眾人都會用石頭打我們，因為他們都認定約翰是先知。”

en: So they answered that they did not know where it came from.
zh: 於是他們回答耶穌：“我們不知道是從哪裡來的。”

en: And Jesus said to them, "Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things."
zh: 耶穌說：“我也不告訴你們，我憑著甚麼權柄作這些事。”

en: And he began to tell the people this parable: "A man planted a vineyard and let it out to tenants and went into another country for a long while.
zh: 於是耶穌向眾人講了這個比喻：“有一個人栽種了一個葡萄園，租給佃戶，就遠行去了很久。

en: When the time came, he sent a servant to the tenants, so that they would give him some of the fruit of the vineyard. But the tenants beat him and sent him away empty-handed.
zh: 到了時候，園主派了一個僕人到佃戶那裡，叫他們把葡萄園當納的果子交給他。佃戶卻打了他，放他空手回去。

en: And he sent another servant. But they also beat and treated him shamefully, and sent him away empty-handed.
zh: 園主又派另一個僕人去，佃戶又打了他，並且侮辱他，也叫他空手回去。

en: And he sent yet a third. This one also they wounded and cast out.
zh: 於是他又派第三個去，佃戶把他打傷，趕他走了。

en: Then the owner of the vineyard said, 'What shall I do? I will send my beloved son; perhaps they will respect him.'
zh: 葡萄園的主人說：‘怎麼辦呢？我要派我的愛子去，也許這一個他們會尊敬的。’

en: But when the tenants saw him, they said to themselves, 'This is the heir. Let us kill him, so that the inheritance may be ours.'
zh: 佃戶一看他，就彼此商議說：‘這是繼承產業的，讓我們殺了他，那麼，產業就成為我們的了。’

en: And they threw him out of the vineyard and killed him. What then will the owner of the vineyard do to them?
zh: 於是他們把園主的愛子推出葡萄園外殺了。主人要怎樣對待他們呢？

en: He will come and destroy those tenants and give the vineyard to others." When they heard this, they said, "Surely not!"
zh: 他要來除掉這些佃戶，把葡萄園租給別人。”他們聽見了就說：“千萬不要有這樣的事發生！”

en: But he looked directly at them and said, "What then is this that is written: "' The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone'?
zh: 耶穌看著他們，說：“那麼， ‘建築工人所棄的石頭， 成了房角的主要石頭’， 這經文是甚麼意思呢？

en: Everyone who falls on that stone will be broken to pieces, and when it falls on anyone, it will crush him."
zh: 凡是跌在那石頭上的，就必摔碎；那石頭掉在誰的身上，就必把他壓得粉碎。”

en: The scribes and the chief priests sought to lay hands on him at that very hour, for they perceived that he had told this parable against them, but they feared the people.
zh: 經學家和祭司長知道這比喻是針對他們說的，當時就想動手拿他，可是又害怕群眾。

en: So they watched him and sent spies, who pretended to be sincere, that they might catch him in something he said, so as to deliver him up to the authority and jurisdiction of the governor.
zh: 經學家和祭司長差派奸細偽裝好人去窺探耶穌，要在他的話上抓著把柄，好把他交由總督全權裁決。

en: So they asked him, "Teacher, we know that you speak and teach rightly, and show no partiality, but truly teach the way of God.
zh: 奸細問他：“老師，我們知道你所說所教的都對，並且不講情面，只照著真理把 神的道教導人。

en: Is it lawful for us to give tribute to Caesar, or not?"
zh: 我們納稅給凱撒可以不可以？”

en: But he perceived their craftiness, and said to them,
zh: 耶穌看透他們的詭計，就對他們說：

en: "Show me a denarius. Whose likeness and inscription does it have?" They said, "Caesar's."
zh: “給我一個銀幣看看，上面是誰的像，誰的名號？”他們說：“凱撒的。”

en: He said to them, "Then render to Caesar the things that are Caesar's, and to God the things that are God's."
zh: 耶穌說：“這樣，凱撒的應當歸給凱撒， 神的應當歸給 神。”

en: And they were not able in the presence of the people to catch him in what he said, but marveling at his answer they became silent.
zh: 他們在群眾面前，不能抓住耶穌的把柄，又希奇他的回答，就沉默下來。

en: There came to him some Sadducees, those who deny that there is a resurrection,
zh: 撒都該人向來否認有復活的事，他們當中有幾個人來問耶穌：

en: and they asked him a question, saying, "Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a man's brother dies, having a wife but no children, the man must take the widow and raise up offspring for his brother.
zh: “老師，摩西曾寫給我們說：‘如果一個人娶了妻子，還沒有兒女就死了，他的弟弟就應當娶他的妻子，為哥哥立後。’

en: Now there were seven brothers. The first took a wife, and died without children.
zh: 從前有兄弟七人，頭一個娶了妻子，沒有孩子就死了。

en: And the second
zh: 第二個、

en: and the third took her, and likewise all seven left no children and died.
zh: 第三個，也娶過她；七個都是這樣，都沒有留下孩子就死了。

en: Afterward the woman also died.
zh: 後來那女人也死了。

en: In the resurrection, therefore, whose wife will the woman be? For the seven had her as wife."
zh: 那麼，這女人在復活的時候，要作哪一個的妻子呢？因為七個人都娶過她。”

en: And Jesus said to them, "The sons of this age marry and are given in marriage,
zh: 耶穌說：“這世界的人又娶又嫁，

en: but those who are considered worthy to attain to that age and to the resurrection from the dead neither marry nor are given in marriage,
zh: 但配得那世界的，又配從死人中復活的人，也不娶也不嫁。

en: for they cannot die anymore, because they are equal to angels and are sons of God, being sons of the resurrection.
zh: 他們和天使一樣，不能再死；既然是復活了的人，就是 神的兒子了。

en: But that the dead are raised, even Moses showed, in the passage about the bush, where he calls the Lord the God of Abraham and the God of Isaac and the God of Jacob.
zh: 至於死人復活的事，摩西在荊棘篇上提過，他稱主是亞伯拉罕的 神、以撒的 神、雅各的 神；

en: Now he is not God of the dead, but of the living, for all live to him."
zh: 神不是死人的 神，而是活人的 神，因為在他那裡的人都是活的。”

en: Then some of the scribes answered, "Teacher, you have spoken well."
zh: 經學家中有幾個說：“老師，你說得好。”

en: For they no longer dared to ask him any question.
zh: 從此他們再不敢問他甚麼了。

en: But he said to them, "How can they say that the Christ is David's son?
zh: 耶穌問他們：“人怎麼能說基督是大衛的子孫呢？

en: For David himself says in the Book of Psalms, "' The Lord said to my Lord, Sit at my right hand,
zh: 大衛自己在詩篇上說： ‘主對我的主說： 你坐在我的右邊，

en: until I make your enemies your footstool.'
zh: 等我使你的仇敵作你的腳凳。’

en: David thus calls him Lord, so how is he his son?"
zh: 大衛既然稱他為主，他又怎會是大衛的子孫呢？”

en: And in the hearing of all the people he said to his disciples,
zh: 眾人聽著的時候，耶穌對門徒說：

en: "Beware of the scribes, who like to walk around in long robes, and love greetings in the marketplaces and the best seats in the synagogues and the places of honor at feasts,
zh: “你們要提防經學家，他們好穿長袍走來走去，喜歡人在巿中心向他們問安，並且喜歡會堂裡的高位、筵席上的首座。

en: who devour widows' houses and for a pretense make long prayers. They will receive the greater condemnation."
zh: 他們吞沒了寡婦的房產，又用冗長的禱告作掩飾。這等人必受更重的刑罰。”
